purpose: Default persistence, memory, and storage growth policy for DreamCo bots before any cloud or paid automation is enabled.

defaults.secrets: never_commit_tokens_or_api_keys; use environment variables or OS keychain
defaults.financial_data: read_only_until_human_approval; no autonomous transfers or purchases
defaults.retention: keep only what the bot needs; user-controlled deletion required

memory_tiers.purpose: Active task memory, live state, recent failures, and short-term bot context.
Useful lessons, approved source notes, completed task summaries, and reusable skill memory.
Compressed audit archives, long-term evidence, historical source snapshots, and legal hold exports.
Searchable memory references for retrieval without storing a giant monolithic embedding file.

partitioning_rules: Never store all bot resources, memories, events, vectors, or source snapshots in one monolithic file.
Every large collection must have a small manifest index and independently loadable shards.
Shard generated libraries by division first, then by bot or month when a division shard reaches its budget.
Shard runtime memory by tenant, division, bot, memory type, and month.
Store binary artifacts, screenshots, documents, and large transcripts as content-addressed files referenced from metadata.
Use append-only event logs for audit evidence, then compact into approved summaries on schedule.
Keep dashboards on summaries and manifests so the UI can load without reading the full memory corpus.
compaction_rules: Summarize old hot memory into warm memory before deleting any task-level detail.
Deduplicate identical source references and keep the best-scored copy.
Compress cold archives and keep checksum, byte size, source, created_at, and retention metadata in the manifest.
Delete or redact expired personal data when retention ends unless legal hold is active.
Keep failure and decision summaries even when raw task scratchpads expire.
Run storage guard checks before merging generated library or dashboard changes.
